page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Operating at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this server includes 64 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in single-core and text processing tasks. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2196.0 and a text processing score of 2919.0, both placing in the top tier. Multi-core performance is also robust, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 16926.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 197.9 GB/sec. Static web serving throughput reached 53.01 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 417.13 tokens/sec using a small 135M parameter model.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highcpu-32 offers a high-compute profile with dedicated CPU allocation, making it highly efficient for CPU-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. Additionally, the lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which may affect I/O-heavy workloads.

This instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as high-performance web serving, text and image processing, software compilation, and lightweight LLM inference. It is also appropriate for batch processing and single-threaded or multi-threaded compression tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized instance designed for CPU-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) running at 2.3 GHz, it features 64 GB of RAM, a 260 MB L3 cache, and no local storage or GPUs. The server exhibits top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2196.0 and strong text processing capabilities. It also delivers high throughput for static web serving at 53.01 GB/sec and efficient multi-threaded decompression. While highly cost-efficient for pure compute tasks due to its dedicated CPU allocation, its 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and lack of local storage present tradeoffs for memory-heavy or high-I/O applications. This instance is best suited for web serving, software compilation, data compression, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance suited for web serving and text processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed cache for demanding web serving and text processing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz.
64 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Top-tier single-core and text processing performance in benchmark testing.
No local storage or bundled GPUs, requiring network-attached storage.
Optimized for high-performance web serving, software compilation, and data compression.
